title: ampelocissus rugosa
intro: Ampelocissus rugosa, also known as the grape creeper, is a type of vine native to tropical Asia, characterized by its woody stems and small, greenish-yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Ampelocissus rugosa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-diarrheal agent. In traditional herbal medicine, Ampelocissus rugosa is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, respiratory infections, and digestive issues.
